text: A friend is one soul abiding in two bodies.
author: Aristotle

text: What is a friend? A single soul dwelling in two bodies.
author: Aristotle

text: Wishing to be friends is quick work, but friendship is a slow ripening fruit.
author: Aristotle

text: Friendship is a single soul dwelling in two bodies.
author: Unknown

text: A real friend is one who walks in when the rest of the world walks out.
author: Walter Winchell

text: Friendship is born at that moment when one person says to another, 'What! You too? I thought I was the only one.'
author: C. S. Lewis

text: Friendship is unnecessary, like philosophy, like art. It has no survival value; rather it is one of those things which give value to survival.
author: C. S. Lewis

text: A friend is someone who knows all about you and still loves you.
author: Elbert Hubbard

text: The friend is the man who knows all about you, and still likes you.
author: Elbert Hubbard

text: True friendship comes when the silence between two people is comfortable.
author: David Tyson

text: A single rose can be my garden; a single friend, my world.
author: Leo Buscaglia

text: Friendship is the only cement that will ever hold the world together.
author: Woodrow Wilson

text: The only way to have a friend is to be one.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: It is one of the blessings of old friends that you can afford to be stupid with them.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: A friend may well be reckoned the masterpiece of nature.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: The glory of friendship is not the outstretched hand, nor the kindly smile, nor the joy of companionship; it is the spiritual inspiration that comes to one when he discovers that someone else believes in him and is willing to trust him.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: The language of friendship is not words but meanings.
author: Henry David Thoreau

text: The most I can do for my friend is simply be his friend.
author: Henry David Thoreau

text: Friends... they cherish one another's hopes. They are kind to one another's dreams.
author: Henry David Thoreau

text: A true friend is one who overlooks your failures and tolerates your success.
author: Doug Larson

text: Friendship improves happiness and abates misery, by the doubling of our joy and the dividing of our grief.
author: Marcus Tullius Cicero

text: A friend is, as it were, a second self.
author: Marcus Tullius Cicero

text: Life is nothing without friendship.
author: Marcus Tullius Cicero

text: The greatest gift of life is friendship, and I have received it.
author: Hubert H. Humphrey

text: There is nothing on this earth more to be prized than true friendship.
author: Thomas Aquinas

text: Friendship is the hardest thing in the world to explain. It's not something you learn in school. But if you haven't learned the meaning of friendship, you really haven't learned anything.
author: Muhammad Ali

text: My best friend is the one who brings out the best in me.
author: Henry Ford

text: A friend to all is a friend to none.
author: Aristotle

text: Walking with a friend in the dark is better than walking alone in the light.
author: Helen Keller

text: I would rather walk with a friend in the dark, than alone in the light.
author: Helen Keller

text: Friends show their love in times of trouble, not in happiness.
author: Euripides

text: One loyal friend is worth ten thousand relatives.
author: Euripides

text: Be slow to fall into friendship; but when thou art in, continue firm and constant.
author: Socrates

text: There are no strangers here; only friends you haven't yet met.
author: William Butler Yeats

text: Think where man's glory most begins and ends, and say my glory was I had such friends.
author: William Butler Yeats

text: Friendship is the golden thread that ties the heart of all the world.
author: John Evelyn

text: A good friend is like a four-leaf clover: hard to find and lucky to have.
author: Irish Proverb

text: The best mirror is an old friend.
author: George Herbert

text: True friends are like diamonds, bright, beautiful, valuable, and always in style.
author: Nicole Richie

text: Friends are the family you choose.
author: Jess C. Scott

text: A loyal friend laughs at your jokes when they're not so good, and sympathizes with your problems when they're not so bad.
author: Arnold H. Glasow

text: True friendship comes when two friends can walk in opposite directions, yet remain side by side.
author: Unknown

text: Good friends are like stars. You don't always see them, but you know they're always there.
author: Unknown

text: Friends are those rare people who ask how we are, and then wait to hear the answer.
author: Ed Cunningham

text: A friend is what the heart needs all the time.
author: Henry van Dyke

text: In the sweetness of friendship let there be laughter, and sharing of pleasures.
author: Khalil Gibran

text: Your friend is your needs answered.
author: Khalil Gibran

text: Let there be no purpose in friendship save the deepening of the spirit.
author: Khalil Gibran

text: Friendship is always a sweet responsibility, never an opportunity.
author: Khalil Gibran

text: It's the friends you can call up at 4 a.m. that matter.
author: Marlene Dietrich

text: Lots of people want to ride with you in the limo, but what you want is someone who will take the bus with you when the limo breaks down.
author: Oprah Winfrey

text: Surround yourself with only people who are going to lift you higher.
author: Oprah Winfrey

text: A friend is someone who gives you total freedom to be yourself.
author: Jim Morrison

text: Friendship is delicate as a glass, once broken it can be fixed but there will always be cracks.
author: Waqar Ahmed

text: Don't walk behind me; I may not lead. Don't walk in front of me; I may not follow. Just walk beside me and be my friend.
author: Albert Camus

text: True friends stab you in the front.
author: Oscar Wilde

text: Anybody can sympathize with the sufferings of a friend, but it requires a very fine nature to sympathize with a friend's success.
author: Oscar Wilde

text: Ultimately the bond of all companionship, whether in marriage or in friendship, is conversation.
author: Oscar Wilde

text: The better part of one's life consists of his friendships.
author: Abraham Lincoln

text: Do I not destroy my enemies when I make them my friends?
author: Abraham Lincoln

text: It is not so much our friends' help that helps us, as the confidence of their help.
author: Epicurus

text: Of all the things which wisdom provides to make us entirely happy, much the greatest is the possession of friendship.
author: Epicurus

text: Each friend represents a world in us, a world possibly not born until they arrive, and it is only by this meeting that a new world is born.
author: Anais Nin

text: The proper office of a friend is to side with you when you are in the wrong. Nearly anybody will side with you when you are in the right.
author: Mark Twain

text: Good friends, good books, and a sleepy conscience: this is the ideal life.
author: Mark Twain

text: To get the full value of joy you must have someone to divide it with.
author: Mark Twain

text: A friend is a gift you give yourself.
author: Robert Louis Stevenson

text: We are all travelers in the wilderness of this world, and the best we can find in our travels is an honest friend.
author: Robert Louis Stevenson

text: There is nothing better than a friend, unless it is a friend with chocolate.
author: Linda Grayson

text: Friendship is the source of the greatest pleasures, and without friends even the most agreeable pursuits become tedious.
author: Thomas Aquinas

text: If you have two friends in your lifetime, you're lucky. If you have one good friend, you're more than lucky.
author: S. E. Hinton

text: Friendship marks a life even more deeply than love. Love risks degenerating into obsession, friendship is never anything but sharing.
author: Elie Wiesel

text: One of the most beautiful qualities of true friendship is to understand and to be understood.
author: Seneca

text: Friendship always benefits; love sometimes injures.
author: Seneca

text: Some people go to priests, others to poetry, I to my friends.
author: Virginia Woolf

text: I would rather walk in the dark with a friend than alone in the light.
author: Unknown

text: A friend in need is a friend indeed.
author: Unknown

text: Side by side or miles apart, real friends are always close to the heart.
author: Unknown

text: Friends are like walls. Sometimes you lean on them, and sometimes it's good just knowing they are there.
author: Unknown

text: A true friend never gets in your way unless you happen to be going down.
author: Arnold H. Glasow

text: Friendship consists in forgetting what one gives and remembering what one receives.
author: Alexandre Dumas

text: There is nothing I would not do for those who are really my friends. I have no notion of loving people by halves; it is not my nature.
author: Jane Austen

text: Friendship is certainly the finest balm for the pangs of disappointed love.
author: Jane Austen

text: Rare as is true love, true friendship is rarer.
author: Jean de La Fontaine

text: Friendship is a sheltering tree.
author: Samuel Taylor Coleridge

text: The friendship that can cease has never been real.
author: Saint Jerome

text: No person is your friend who demands your silence, or denies your right to grow.
author: Alice Walker

text: Growing apart doesn't change the fact that for a long time we grew side by side; our roots will always be tangled.
author: Ally Condie

text: Piglet sidled up to Pooh from behind. 'Pooh!' he whispered. 'Yes, Piglet?' 'Nothing,' said Piglet, taking Pooh's paw. 'I just wanted to be sure of you.'
author: A. A. Milne

text: We'll be friends forever, won't we, Pooh? asked Piglet. Even longer, Pooh answered.
author: A. A. Milne

text: It is not what we have, but who we have in our lives that matters.
author: Unknown

text: A sweet friendship refreshes the soul.
author: Unknown

text: Some souls just understand each other upon meeting.
author: N. R. Hart

text: Find a group of people who challenge and inspire you; spend a lot of time with them, and it will change your life.
author: Amy Poehler

text: There's not a word yet for old friends who've just met.
author: Jim Henson

text: A day without a friend is like a pot without a single drop of honey left inside.
author: A. A. Milne

text: In everyone's life, at some time, our inner fire goes out. It is then burst into flame by an encounter with another human being. We should all be thankful for those people who rekindle the inner spirit.
author: Albert Schweitzer

text: Remember that the most valuable antiques are dear old friends.
author: H. Jackson Brown Jr.

text: Wherever we are, it is our friends that make our world.
author: Henry Drummond

text: Life is partly what we make it, and partly what it is made by the friends we choose.
author: Tennessee Williams
